1988November 29, 1988

🇯🇵 Tokyo, Japan

Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Tokyo, Japan, November 29, 1988 -- December 9, 1988

Meeting URNurn:iso:tc:184:sc:4:meeting:plenary-1988-12-tokyo-japan

14 Resolutions

42Plenary
Direct WG 1 to develop plan for future STEP development
SC 4 directs that WG 1 develop a plan for the future development of STEP. The working plan is to be approved by SC 4 before October 1989 by mail ballot.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
41Plenary
Direct secretariat to establish stronger liaisons
SC 4 directs the secretariat to establish stronger liaisons with other international bodies, especially the appropriate bodies in the areas of AEC, FEM and Electrical/Electronics. Member bodies sha...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
40Plenary
Encourage implementation and validation of STEP DP
SC 4 asks its member bodies and liaisons to encourage implementation and validation of the STEP Draft Proposal or parts of it as soon as majority approval has been gained. This encouragement is to ...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
39Plenary
Direct WG 1 to study CAD Standard Parts initiatives
SC 4 directs WG 1 to undertake a study of all existing initiatives relating to the use of CAD Standard Parts and to recommend any action required for international harmonisation and/or standardizat...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
38Plenary
Direct WG 1 to develop material properties topical model
SC 4 directs WG 1 to develop a topical model that deals with aspects of material properties with relationship to design, testing, and analysis of products, which shall be added to the STEP Informat...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
37Plenary
Direct WG 1 to develop product life cycle support model
SC 4 directs WG 1 to develop a topical model that deals with aspects of product life cycle support. This model shall be added to the STEP Information Model.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
36Plenary
Direct WG 1 to develop motion and kinematics topical model
SC 4 directs WG 1 to develop a topical model that deals with aspects of motion and kinematics as is required for the representation of products for which these aspects are essential (mechanisms, in...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
35Plenary
Direct WG 1 to seek approval before initiating new models
SC 4 directs WG 1 to obtain SC 4 approval for the initiation of activities regarding the development of new topical and application models for STEP.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
34Plenary
Form application profiles as separate parts of STEP
SC 4 resolves that application profiles of STEP shall form separate parts of the STEP standard, in order to provide a basis for conformance testing.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
32Plenary
Direct WG 1 to consider logical subdivision of STEP
SC 4 directs WG 1 to consider a logical subdivision of the STEP standard into an open set of consistent standards. STEP shall be based on one single, complete, implementation-independent Product In...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
31Plenary
Appoint Curtis Parks as Chairman of the Editing Committee
SC 4 appoints Curtis Parks of NIST, USA to serve in the capacity of Chairman of the Editing Committee until that committee has convened and established their own chairman. The Chairman shall conven...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
30Plenary
Establish an editing committee for the STEP Draft Proposal
SC 4 establishes an editing committee to finalize the text of the Draft Proposal for submission as a Draft International Standard and maintain configuration control so that all future changes be do...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
29Plenary
Approve registration of STEP as Draft Proposal (DP)
SC 4 approves the registration of STEP as an ISO Draft Proposal (DP) in accordance with ISO Directives Section 6.4.1.1. The DP comprises the following WG 1 documents: * N279 (Physical File Structur...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
Acclamation
Acclamation
SC 4 and WG 1 would like to record a vote of thanks to Japan as host nation for the excellent facilities and support for the November 1988 meeting.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
ISO/TC 184/SC 4

Industrial data

Standardization of the content, meaning, structure, representation and quality management of information for engineered products throughout their lifecycle.

Committee

  • Secretariat: ANSI (Secretariat)
  • Chair: Kenneth Swope
  • Established: 1984
  • Standards: 822 published
  • Members: 21 (P), 14 (O)
